title: Pods, Multi-Container Composition, Lifecycle, Termination, and Debug Containers · Stakeholder Platform Mission
summary: A municipal service-status team. The team needs an immutable multi-container Pod design with config, startup/readiness/liveness intent, bounded resources, graceful termination, structured status evidence, and a changed-failure rehearsal. Planned artifact: Deliver Accessible Status Workload Rollout with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Trace desired state, observed state, controller decisions, idempotent actions, status updates, and eventual convergence through a changed failure.
Preview and apply declarative changes while tracing field managers, conflicts, pruning scope, server-side apply, and changed status.
Choose init, app, native sidecar, adapter, ambassador, and helper containers from startup, restart, ownership, shutdown, and resource contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project K8s Declarative Reconciliation Loop
steps.instruction:

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-hooks-signals-termination-103rs7
  - competency: k8s-hooks-signals-termination
    artifact: pod.yaml
    decision: hooks signals termination
    environment: three-node dual-stack staging cluster across two zones
    fault-injection: remove DNS egress from an otherwise ready workload
    retained-proof: object UID generation observedGeneration and status conditions
    case-key: 103rs7
    change: |
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Pod
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      initContainers: [schema-check]
      containers: [api, telemetry-sidecar]
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous -c api
    failure: Reject durable-Pod assumptions, unbounded sidecars, swallowed signals, missing previous logs, or debug mutation presented as image repair.
    repair: Assign container ownership, bound startup and shutdown, capture states and exit causes, and prove a clean replacement preserves required behavior.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
